finding.contract_claim: Symbol `get_file_tools` is declared but appears unused — no references were found outside its own declaration.
finding.observed_behavior: No callers or importers of `get_file_tools` were found in the fixture project; consumer modules import the get_*_tool_definitions() variants instead, so this wrapper is never invoked even though the TOOL constant it returns is alive.

finding.triage_reasoning: Reality: `get_file_tools()` at L311-313 returns raw `list[dict]`. The only active consumer pattern in this codebase is the `get_*_tool_definitions()` variants that return `list[ToolDefinition]`; `shadow.py` imports and calls `get_file_tool_definitions()` (confirmed in Claim 5 evidence). The sweep of 6 files found zero callers of `get_file_tools`. Significance: dead wrapper clutters the module and may confuse maintainers. Actionability: remove the function.
finding.suggested_fix: Remove `get_file_tools()` (L311-313). Its TOOL constant (`SUBMIT_SHADOW_DOC_TOOL`) is alive via `get_file_tool_definitions()`; only the raw-dict wrapper is dead.
